query: What books by Moojan Momen do you have?
response: The library holds 146 works by Moojan Momen. Notable titles include [Mazhar-i Ilahi (Manifestation of God)](https://oceanlibrary.com), [Memorials of the Faithful: The Democratization of Sainthood](https://oceanlibrary.com), and [Shoghi Effendi's Translation of Terms Related to Law in Bahá’í Scripture](https://oceanlibrary.com). Momen's work explores the historical context of the Bahá'í Faith, such as the influence of Bahá'í leaders on 19th-century reform movements. He notes that ["the Bahá'í leaders could have had a significant impact"](https://oceanlibrary.com) on these debates, particularly through `Abdu'l-Bahá's writings, which were widely read among Iranian intellectuals.

response: The library holds 522 documents by Bahá'u'lláh. Among these are notable works like the [Súrah of the Arabs](https://bahai-library.com/pdf/uhj/uhj_mariner_1996.pdf), [Suffering of the Exalted Letters](https://bahai-library.com/pdf/uhj/uhj_mariner_1996.pdf), and the [Tablet of Consolation](https://bahai-library.com/pdf/uhj/uhj_mariner_1996.pdf). In his writings, Bahá'u'lláh conveys profound spiritual truths, such as in the Tablet of the Holy Mariner, where he speaks of ["the Ancient Beauty hath consented to be bound with chains"](https://bahai-library.com/pdf/uhj/uhj_mariner_1996.pdf), illustrating his sacrifice and dedication.
